message:
test(frankenpandas): add Missing Data Handling test + fix README dropna_with_threshold arity (br-jl1go / fd90.172)

fd90.172: integration test for the README's 'Missing Data Handling'
section (lines 944-994) caught an additional fd90.104 follow-on
defect — dropna_with_threshold takes 2 args (threshold + Option
subset), not 1. fd90.104 fixed the rename from dropna_with_thresh
to dropna_with_threshold but kept the single-arg form, which still
wouldn't compile.

Two changes:
1. README line 995: dropna_with_threshold(3)? → dropna_with_threshold(3, None)?
   with inline note about the (threshold, optional subset) shape.
2. Integration test 'readme_missing_data_handling_compiles_and_runs'
   that mirrors the example. Builds a Series with NaN values and a
   sparse DataFrame and exercises:
     Detection: series.isna(), series.notna(), series.count(),
                series.hasnans()
     Filling: series.fillna, ffill (None/Some), bfill (Some), interpolate
     Dropping: df.dropna(), df.dropna_with_options(DropNaHow::All|Any),
               df.dropna_with_threshold(2, None), df.dropna_columns()

Verified:
- cargo test -p frankenpandas — 7 lib + 16 integration + 2 doctests
  all pass

The new test caught the dropna_with_threshold arity miss in CI rather
than waiting for a user copy-paste.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-jk7v","title":"Add dataframe head/tail zero-n strict conformance packets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:35:53.824224416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:40:17.213133039Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:40:17.211037262Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-jkhg","title":"[HIGH] fuzz: 3 of 5 fp-expr string-input pub fns lack fuzz targets (query_str, query_str_with_locals, parse_expr)","description":"Applying /testing-fuzzing skill Rule 3: "Every pub fn accepting &[u8], &str, impl Read from untrusted sources MUST have a fuzz target."

**Observation.** fp-expr exposes FOUR distinct string-accepting public APIs:

  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:314: pub fn eval_str(s, df, policy, ledger) -> Result<Series>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:323: pub fn eval_str_with_locals(s, df, locals, policy, ledger) -> Result<Series>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:338: pub fn query_str(s, df, policy, ledger) -> Result<DataFrame>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:347: pub fn query_str_with_locals(s, df, locals, policy, ledger) -> Result<DataFrame>
  fp-expr/src/lib.rs:737: pub fn parse_expr(s) -> Result<Expr>

The existing `fuzz_dataframe_eval` target (fuzz_dataframe_eval_bytes at fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:5431) covers `eval_str` only. The other three pub fns have zero fuzz coverage despite accepting untrusted expression strings with identical-or-worse attack surface:

1. `query_str` produces a DataFrame (filter semantics) rather than a Series. Different code path post-parse: the result must survive a boolean-mask projection + index preservation. Bugs there don't surface via eval_str.
2. `parse_expr` is the lowest level — it returns an AST `Expr` directly. If parse_expr panics or infinite-loops on a crafted string, EVERY higher-level eval/query caller inherits the bug. It's the narrowest fuzz target per skill Rule 2 ("fuzz the parser, not the application").
3. `query_str_with_locals` extends query_str with a BTreeMap<String, Scalar> locals argument. The locals-propagation path has its own opportunity for bugs — e.g. a locals key that happens to match a frame column name has precedence semantics that could be tested only via fuzz.

**Skill rule violated:** Rule 3 (every pub fn in the untrusted-input class must have a fuzz target). Today: 4 of 5 such expr pub fns lack fuzz coverage.

**Severity.** HIGH — expression evaluation is a security-sensitive surface. pandas' `df.query("col > 5")` has a documented history of allowing broader expression constructs than users realize (pandas's `numexpr` has had CVEs over eval of arbitrary attribute access). Our surface is smaller but nothing proves absence of infinite loops or panics on crafted strings.

**Fix shape:**

1. `fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_parse_expr.rs` — minimal wrapper around `fp_conformance::fuzz_parse_expr_bytes`.
2. `fp-conformance::fuzz_parse_expr_bytes(data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), String>`:
   - Convert data to lossy UTF-8, clamp to 256 chars.
   - Call parse_expr(&s). Assert no panic. No AST shape invariant (parse_expr returns Err freely).
   - Size guard: `if data.len() > 4096 { return; }`.
3. `fuzz_query_str_bytes(data: &[u8])`:
   - Same input projection.
   - Build a tiny frame via `fuzz_series_from_bytes("x", data)` (reuse existing helper).
   - Call `query_str(&s, &frame, &RuntimePolicy::strict(), &mut EvidenceLedger::new())`.
   - Assert no panic. If Ok, assert `result.index().len() <= frame.index().len()` (query can only shrink).
4. `fuzz_query_str_with_locals_bytes`:
   - Same as query_str_bytes but constructs a 3-key locals BTreeMap from the input bytes (first byte picks the key name from ["x", "col", "__secret__"], value picks from Scalar::Int64(0), Int64(1), Utf8("test"), Null(Null)).
   - Asserts locals propagation doesn't panic.

Seed corpus (5 minimum per target):
- empty string
- syntactically valid trivial: "1"
- common: "col1 > 5"
- boundary: deeply nested "(((((((((x)))))))))" — tests parser recursion depth
- adversarial: Unicode with zero-width joiner + invalid UTF-8 tail

Each target adds to fuzz/Cargo.toml as [[bin]]. Target exec/s: skill's parser floor is 1000/s. These should hit it easily.

**Related:**
- br-zjme (fuzz CI) — these 3 new targets plug into the same CI gate once zjme lands.
- br-liai (differential vs pandas) — eval/query differential fuzz is the natural follow-up: same input → pandas `df.eval()` / `df.query()` → assert semantic_eq with our result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:48:19.803925628Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:49:06.137247363Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:49:06.134768923Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fp-expr","fuzz","pub-api","review"]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-jl1go","title":"fd90.172: add Missing Data Handling integration test (locks in fd90.104 dropna_with_threshold)","description":"fd90.74-171 follow-on. The README's 'Missing Data Handling' section (lines 944-994) covers Detection / Filling / Dropping but no integration test exercises it. The fd90.104 fix renamed dropna_with_thresh to dropna_with_threshold; the test would lock that in too.

Fix: add 'readme_missing_data_handling_compiles_and_runs' which mirrors the example. Build a Series with NaN values + a DataFrame with sparse columns and exercise:
  Detection: series.isna(), series.notna(), series.count(), series.hasnans()
  Filling: series.fillna, ffill, bfill, interpolate
  Dropping: df.dropna(), df.dropna_with_options, df.dropna_with_threshold, df.dropna_columns

Pure additive test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:59:30.573207704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:02:15.244079840Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:02:15.241266661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Land any divergence as [HIGH] review bead with failing input + actual + expected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T03:21:45.625363946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:31:47.388388624Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:31:47.386541617Z","close_reason":"Added 9 live-oracle Index parity matrix cases and oracle monotonic handlers","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","index","parity-matrix"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-jmmo","title":"fd90.53: SqlInspector::reflect_all_tables for whole-database introspection","description":"Add SqlInspector::reflect_all_tables(schema) -> Result<Vec<SqlReflectedTable>, IoError>. Iterates over tables(schema) and reflect_table() each one, returning a vec of bundles. Useful for whole-database introspection in one ergonomic call. Skips tables that table_schema returns None for (race condition where a table existed at list time but not at reflect time). Pure delegation. Tests cover: empty database returns empty vec, populated DB returns one bundle per user table, multi-schema backend stub propagates schema arg.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:30:00.517098293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:33:05.741694250Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:33:05.739207271Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["api","fd90","review","sql"]}
